server.name: ai.agenticterminal/directory
server.description: Verified merchants accepting agentic payments on Lightning/L402/BOLT12/USDT — search, verify, pay.
server.title: Agentic Terminal Directory

ai.agenticterminal.anonymous_access: Reads (search, get, verify) require no credential. Bring your own wallet/rails and transact directly with the merchant — the directory is never in the payment path.
credibility: Counterparty-verified, not self-claimed: Bitrefill's Tier-2 status comes from a real autonomous-agent Lightning purchase, preimage-verified on the record. Proof a transaction settled, not a marketing checkbox.
Not a static list: verify_payment_endpoint live-probes a rail and is explicit when a rail is attestation-only rather than rubber-stamping it. The directory tells you what it actually knows.
example_calls: search_merchants({ rail: "lightning", category: "gift-cards" })  ->  Bitrefill (Tier 2) ranked first
verify_payment_endpoint({ merchant_id: "bitrefill", rail: "lightning" })  ->  live status + evidence
ai.agenticterminal.pitch: An agent-callable directory of real merchants you can pay over Lightning, L402, BOLT12 or USDT — each carrying an Observer Protocol trust tier. Discover anonymously with no credential or signup, inspect a merchant's payment surface, and verify a rail is live before you send funds. Built for autonomous agents that bring their own wallet and rails.

tools.description: Filter merchants by rail, chain, category, trust tier and accepts_usdc; returns ranked summaries plus your resolved auth state.
tools.name: search_merchants

tools.description: Full record for one merchant: every rail, payment endpoints, agent_endpoints (MCP/REST/API docs) and any OP attestation.
tools.name: get_merchant

tools.description: Live, rail-specific health check of a merchant's payment surface before you transact — and honest about probe-able vs attestation-only rails.
tools.name: verify_payment_endpoint

tools.description: Category taxonomy with live merchant counts.
tools.name: list_categories

tools.description: Supported rails and USDT chains with live merchant counts.
tools.name: list_rails

tools.description: Your resolved credential/auth state and rate-limit tier.
tools.name: whoami

server.name: ai.com.mcp/contabo
server.description: Contabo API (v1.0.0) as MCP tools for cloud provisioning, and management. Powered by HAPI MCP server
server.title: Contabo (VPS) MCP Server

headers.description: Bearer token for authenticating with the Contabo API. Obtain via the documented token endpoint.
headers.name: Authorization

headers.description: Optional request ID for tracing (recommended for support/debugging)
headers.name: x-request-id

examples.config: Add 'https://contabo.run.mcp.com.ai/mcp' as a remote MCP server in your MCP client. Ensure ACCESS_TOKEN is set in Authorization argument.
examples.description: Connect to the Contabo MCP server to manage cloud resources (VPS, VDS, Object Storage, etc.)
examples.name: Basic Connection

examples.description: List all your Contabo compute instances using the MCP tools
examples.name: Get Instances
examples.note: Ask the MCP client (i.e.: https://chat.mcp.com.ai) to invoke the 'listInstances' tool to retrieve your VPS and VDS instances

examples.description: Obtain ACCESS_TOKEN using documented OAuth2 flow (see docs and example curl in contabo.md)
examples.name: Authentication

notes: This MCP server exposes the full Contabo API as MCP tools for cloud automation.
Authentication is required for all operations. Obtain credentials from the Contabo Customer Control Panel.
Download the full API specification from https://api.contabo.com, or from the HAPI MCP server documentation: https://docs.mcp.com.ai/servers-apis/openapi/
See https://api.contabo.com/#section/Introduction and https://contabo.com/en-us/contabo-api/ for full API documentation.
Run your own HAPI MCP server using Docker: docker run -p 3030:3030  -v ~/.hapi:/app/.hapi serve contabo --port 3030 --headless --url https://api.contabo.com
Or the HAPI MCP CLI: hapi serve contabo --port 3030 --headless --url https://api.contabo.com --openapi https://docs.mcp.com.ai/apis/openapi/contabo.json
Documentation: https://docs.mcp.com.ai/deployment/

server.name: ai.com.mcp/hapi-mcp
server.description: HAPI MCP server: Dynamically exposes OpenAPI REST APIs as MCP tools for AI assistants
server.title: HAPI MCP Server

headers.description: Authorization token for accessing the MCP server
headers.name: Authorization

runtimeArguments.description: Port mapping for the host to container (e.g., '3030:3030'). Adjust second port if changing internal container port (HAPI_PORT)
runtimeArguments.name: -p

runtimeArguments.description: Volume mapping for HAPI configuration directory
runtimeArguments.name: -v

packageArguments.description: Command to start the HAPI MCP server
packageArguments.name: serve

packageArguments.description: The name of the project/API to serve (e.g., 'petstore'). Optional when using HAPI_OPENAPI environment variable
packageArguments.name: projectName

packageArguments.description: The port to listen on inside the container
packageArguments.name: --port

packageArguments.description: Run in headless mode (serve MCP-only, no business logic)
packageArguments.name: --headless

packageArguments.description: The server provides REST API's business logic AND also exposes them as MCP tools
packageArguments.name: --mcp

packageArguments.description: Enable development mode with verbose logging
packageArguments.name: --dev

packageArguments.description: The full URL for the backend API server (overrides OpenAPI spec servers). Uses OAS server URL if not provided
packageArguments.name: --url

packageArguments.description: OpenAPI spec file path or URL to use instead of loading from HAPI_HOME
packageArguments.name: --openapi

packageArguments.description: Comma-separated list of allowed origins for CORS (e.g., 'http://localhost:3000,https://example.com')
packageArguments.name: --cors

packageArguments.description: Comma-separated list of OAS configuration files to apply (can be URLs)
packageArguments.name: --filename

packageArguments.description: Path to SSL certificate file for HTTPS. Volume mount required if using Docker. https://docs.mcp.com.ai/components/hapi-server/hapi-cli#running-with-tls-https
packageArguments.name: --cert

packageArguments.description: Path to SSL key file for HTTPS. Volume mount required if using Docker
packageArguments.name: --key

environmentVariables.description: Fully Qualified Domain Name for the HAPI server (used in MCP tool URLs)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_FQDN

environmentVariables.description: Directory path for HAPI configuration and specs. Should be mounted as a volume in Docker
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_HOME

environmentVariables.description: The name of the project/API (alternative to positional argument)
environmentVariables.name: PROJECT_NAME

environmentVariables.description: OpenAPI spec URL or path (alternative to --openapi flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_OPENAPI

environmentVariables.description: Backend API base URL (alternative to --url flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_URL

environmentVariables.description: Server port (alternative to --port flag) - this is the internal container port
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_PORT

environmentVariables.description: Enable headless mode (alternative to --headless flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_HEADLESS

environmentVariables.description: Enable MCP mode (alternative to --mcp flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_MCP

environmentVariables.description: Enable development mode (alternative to --dev flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_DEV

environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated allowed CORS origins (alternative to --cors flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_CORS

environmentVariables.description: Comma-separated configuration files (alternative to --filename flag)
environmentVariables.name: HAPI_CONFIG_FILES

environmentVariables.description: Node environment (development, production). 'development' enables verbose logging and debugging features.
environmentVariables.name: NODE_ENV

HAPI_FQDN.description: Fully Qualified Domain Name for the HAPI server

examples.command: docker run -p 3030:3030 -v ~/.hapi:/app/.hapi hapimcp/hapi-cli:0.6.0 serve petstore --port 3030 --headless --url https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3
examples.description: Save specs in ~/.hapi/specs. Download example if not present: https://petstore3.swagger.io/api/v3/swagger.json
examples.name: Quick Start with a local API spec

examples.command: docker run -p 3030:3030 -e HAPI_OPENAPI=https://api.example.com/openapi.json -e HAPI_URL=https://api.example.com hapimcp/hapi-cli:0.6.0 serve my-api --headless
examples.description: Serve a custom API using environment variables
examples.name: Custom OpenAPI with Environment Variables

examples.description: Run with persistent volumes and custom specs
examples.name: Docker Compose Setup

Public discovery endpoints are available without authentication.
Rate limits may apply.
For self-hosting, run your own HAPI MCP server using:
Docker: docker run -p 3030:3030  -v ~/.hapi:/app/.hapi serve registry --port 3030 --headless --url https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io

The HAPI MCP CLI: hapi serve registry --port 3030 --headless --url https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io --openapi https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/raw/refs/heads/main/docs/reference/api/openapi.yaml

Deploy to Cloudflare: hapi deploy --name mcp-registry --openapi https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/registry/raw/refs/heads/main/docs/reference/api/openapi.yaml --url https://registry.modelcontextprotocol.io
Documentation: https://docs.mcp.com.ai/deployment/

examples.description: Create a character sprite with specific art style
examples.name: Generate Pixel Art Sprite
examples.note: createImage with image_type='sprite', art_style='Pixel Art (16-Bit)', perspective='Side-Scroll'

examples.description: Generate an app icon for your game
examples.name: Create Isometric Game Icon
examples.note: createImage with image_type='icon', perspective='Isometric', aspect_ratio='ar_1_1'

examples.description: Transform a 2D sprite into a 3D GLB model with PBR textures
examples.name: Convert Sprite to 3D Model
examples.note: create3DModel with texture_type='pbr' returns model_url (.glb) and 4 snapshot images

examples.description: Animate a character with a walk cycle
examples.name: Create Walking Animation
examples.note: animateSprite with motion_prompt='walking cycle', loop=true, frames=16

examples.description: Create animated visual effects
examples.name: Generate Attack VFX
examples.note: animateSprite with image_type='sprite-vfx', motion_prompt='fire explosion effect'

examples.description: Generate a short video from a screenshot
examples.name: Create Game Trailer Clip
examples.note: createVideo with duration=5, prompt='camera slowly zooms in'

examples.description: Create a sci-fi weapon sound effect
examples.name: Generate Laser Sound
examples.note: createSoundEffect with description='futuristic laser gun firing, sci-fi'

examples.description: Generate background music for combat
examples.name: Create Battle Music
examples.note: createMusic with description='epic orchestral battle theme, fast-paced, intense'

examples.description: Create a unique voice for a game character
examples.name: Generate Character Voice
examples.note: createVoice with voice_description='gruff old warrior', text='Prepare for battle!'

notes: Credit-based pricing: images (0.5), 3D models (3), animations (5), videos (5-15), audio (3)
Supports multiple art styles: Pixel Art, Low Poly, Cartoonish, Stylized 3D, and more
3D models output as GLB with optional PBR textures
Spritesheets support variable frame counts (4-64) and sizes (64-256px)
Long-running operations (3D models, animations) may take 60-120 seconds

server.description: Non-custodial Solana swap & limit order engine for AI agents.

environmentVariables.description: Solana base58-encoded private key. Local signing only, never transmitted. See SECURITY.md.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_PRIVATE_KEY

environmentVariables.description: Optional RPC endpoint override (default https://api.mainnet-beta.solana.com).
environmentVariables.name: SOLANA_RPC_URL

environmentVariables.description: Optional override for the server public key used to verify order_filled ed25519 signatures.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_SERVER_PUBKEY

environmentVariables.description: Optional second server public key accepted during key rotation.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_SERVER_PUBKEY_NEXT

environmentVariables.description: Default 'true'. Verify server signatures on order_filled / twap_execution.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_REQUIRE_SERVER_SIGNATURE

environmentVariables.description: Default 'true'. Verify server signatures on order_created.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_REQUIRE_ORDER_CREATED_SIGNATURE

environmentVariables.description: Default 'false'. When 'true', write-action tools short-circuit and return { dry_run: true, ... } instead of calling the API.
environmentVariables.name: TRADEROUTER_DRY_RUN

headers.description: Bearer authorization. headers.name: Authorization

examples.description: Add a food entry by describing what you ate
examples.name: Log a meal
examples.note: Say 'I had a turkey sandwich with avocado for lunch' and the AI will extract calories and macros

examples.description: Get a summary of today's nutrition
examples.name: Check daily progress
examples.note: Ask 'What did I eat today?' or 'How many calories have I had?'

examples.description: Search through past food entries
examples.name: Query meal history
examples.note: Ask 'When was the last time I had pizza?' or 'Show me my protein intake this week'

notes: OAuth 2.1 authentication required - users must sign up or sign in via Calories Club account
Tools: list_entries, add_entry, update_entry, delete_entry, get_day_summary

Privacy-first: photos and audio are processed and never stored on servers

io.modelcontextprotocol.registry/publish.longDescription: ClawSpan ShardLink exposes an MCP surface so LLM-driven agents can discover, claim, and complete paid workspace tasks. Call initialize first, then tools/list to enumerate available capabilities, then tools/call with the tool's name + arguments to invoke. All mutating tools respect lease and idempotency contracts; anonymous discovery (capability graph, workspace directory, pricing) is always available.

quickstart.description: If you are a new agent: fetch /.well-known/roaming-agent.json to discover the full onboarding surface, then call /v1/agents/self-register with a wallet signature or attested service token. After registration, call tools/list via the MCP endpoint and begin claiming sandbox tasks.

resources.description: Machine-readable catalog of every MCP tool + A2A action with path, method, auth, lease, and idempotency metadata.
